title: Changelog
text: Date: 2026-06-22 Major data updates ClinVar (2026-06) dbNSFP (v5.3) CIViC (2026-06-20) GENCODE v49 (VEP v115) Variant classification updates gene-specific allele frequency thresholds different criteria (BA1/BS1/PM2/PM1/PM4) relying allele frequencies one specified subpopulation, rather considering frequencies across gnomAD subpopulations (groupmax approach, recommended ClinGen SVI working group) PM2 supporting given weight accumulating pathogenicity score due limited evidence pathogenicity mere rarity refined handling PVS1 criterion loss--function (LOF) variants according PVS1 decision tree revised MaxEntScan (MES) thresholds LOF variants neighbourhood canonical splice donor/acceptor sites. virtual panel genes HTML report display alphabetic order, ignoring confidence levels (PanelApp genes) New column ACMG_CODE TSV output files, indicating ACMG criteria met variant (‘|’-separated list) Renamed column CPSR_CLASSIFICATION_SOURCE → ASSERTION_AUTHORITY TSV output better reflect role (values: ClinVar CPSR) Renamed column CANCER_PHENOTYPE CLINVAR_PHENOTYPE_CANCER TSV output, clarify annotation based ClinVar phenotype associations Added new column ASSERTION_RATIONALE TSV output, providing human-readable explanation given assertion authority chosen (e.g. “Novel variant (absent ClinVar)”, “ClinVar review status: 2 gold stars”, “Conflicting ClinVar interpretations”) Removed --pop_gnomad (previously required select single gnomAD subpopulation, e.g. nfe) --maf_upper_threshold (global MAF filter) Replaced single argument --max_af_gnomad, applies upper frequency threshold across gnomAD subpopulations rather one selected population — consistent ClinGen SVI groupmax approach already used internally BA1/BS1/PM2 criteria 0: ClinVar fully trusted; CPSR overrides conflicted records (default) 1: CPSR overrides zero gold star ClinVar records 2: CPSR overrides zero- single gold star ClinVar records 3: CPSR overrides low-star non-cancer-phenotype ClinVar records 4: CPSR always classifies, ClinVar records never take precedence Interactive HTML report tables migrated DT (DT::datatable) reactable (reactable::reactable): classification, secondary findings, pharmacogenomics, biomarker, GWAS variant tables now use reactable Tables feature collapsible detail row per variant full annotation context, reducing visual clutter main view Dedicated hidden search index column aggregates nested evidence text fields (HTML biomarker tables) full-text search across evidence items Excel workbook — SETTINGS DATA_VERSIONS sheets SETTINGS — key run parameters three-column SECTION / PARAMETER / VALUE layout, covering: CPSR/PCGR versions, genome assembly, sample ID, variant classification settings (gnomAD MAF threshold, ClinVar trust level, secondary findings, pharmacogenomics findings, GWAS hits, noncoding filter), VEP settings (transcript set, pick order) DATA_VERSIONS — one row per reference dataset used run, columns DATABASE / VERSION / DESCRIPTION / URL / LICENSE; mirrors “Dataset versions” callout HTML Documentation section Infrastructure: major refactor GitHub Actions workflow moving gitflow development mode, main branch stable releases, dev branch development also requires moving required rattler format conda recipe switch conda-incubator/setup-miniconda (mamba-org/setup-micromamba) pkgdown’s auto development mode allows us keep dev website documentation latest development version cpsr, now available https://sigven.github.io/cpsr/dev/ (previously stable versions available website)

text: multiple options related Rmarkdown output now deprecated Users need download assembly-specific VEP cache separately PCGR/CPSR, provide path new required argument --vep_dir cpsr command Re-engineered data bundle generation pipeline HTML report overview contents data bundle shipped reference data , also available (grch38). Biomarkers matched much comprehensively previous versions, matching genomic level, codon, exon, amino acid gene level (principal non-principal transcript matches) bugs retrieval secondary variant findings fixed

title: Variant classification
text: CPSR assign standard classification label (P, LP, VUS, LB, B) aggregated variant pathogenicity score (CPSR_PATHOGENICITY_SCORE)? Optimal thresholds conversion pathogenicity scores categorical variant classification calibrated high-quality ClinVar-classified variants (minimum two gold stars respect review status) core set cancer predisposition genes (see plot ).  following thresholds currently used assign classifications based pathogenicity scores: Although final CPSR pathogenicity score per variant derived summation matching evidence scores/weights, certain weights downgraded particular contexts. Specifically, weight provided supportive computational predictions (e.g. PP3, BP4) count towards final pathogenicity score weight provided criteria. Also, variant single (non-strong, .e. moderate/supporting) pathogenic evidence criterion matching, yet reaching CPSR’s likely pathogenic score threshold, downgraded VUS order avoid overclassification based limited evidence (line ACMG/AMP guidelines). Note score thresholds may subject change future CPSR releases, continue refine optimize classification algorithm based emerging evidence user feedback.

title: Cancer Predisposition Sequencing Reporter (CPSR)
text: Cancer Predisposition Sequencing Reporter (CPSR) computational workflow interprets DNA sequence variants identified next-generation sequencing context cancer predisposition. CPSR accepts query file germline variant calls (SNVs/InDels) single sample (.e. cancer patient), encoded VCF format. comprehensive gene variant annotation procedures, CPSR offers following functionalities user: Flexible selection cancer predisposition genes subject analysis reporting - use virtual gene panels Variant classification (Pathogenic Benign) dedicated implementation ACMG/AMP guidelines Detection germline biomarkers - prognosis, diagnosis, drug sensitivity/resistance cancer Optional detection reporting variants related adverse events/toxicity common cancer chemotherapies Optional reporting secondary/incidental findings (ACMG recommendations) Interactive HTML output report detailed variant information, gene annotations, external links relevant databases CPSR workflow integrated framework underlies Personal Cancer Genome Reporter - PCGR. PCGR intended reporting analysis somatic variants detected tumor, CPSR intended reporting ranking germline variants protein-coding genes implicated cancer predisposition inherited cancer syndromes. Four snapshots sections quarto-based cancer predisposition genome report (artificial sample, findings usual):
